volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Children of Heracles (Section 552 - Section 680)

sections.label: Section 552
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah , this is even nobler than thy former word ; that was matchless , but thou dost now surpass

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: τόλμῃ τε τόλμαν καὶ λόγῳ χρηστῷ λόγον .
sections.translation: thy bravery and noble speech . I cannot bid , will not forbid thy dying , Omy daughter ! for [*] by thy death thou dost thy brothers serve .

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: σοφῶς κελεύεις · μὴ τρέσῃς μιάσματος
sections.translation: A cautious bidding thine ! Fear not to take a stain of guilt from me ; only let me die as one whose death is free .

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ἕπου δέ , πρέσβυ · σῇ γὰρ ἐνθανεῖν χερὶ
sections.translation: Follow me , old friend , for in thy arms I fain would die ; stand by and veil my body with my robe , for I will go even to the dreadful doom of sacrifice , seeing whose daughter I avow myself .

sections.label: Section 564
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δυναίμην σῷ παρεστάναι μόρῳ .
sections.translation: I cannot stand by and see thee bleed .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἀλλὰ τοῦδε χρῇζε , μή μʼ ἐν ἀρσένων ,
sections.translation: At least do thou beg me this boon of the king , that I may breathe out my life in women ’ s arms instead of men ’ s .

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ , ὦ τάλαινα παρθένων , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: It shall be so , unhappy maid ; for this were shame to me to refuse thee honour due , for many reasons : because thou hast a soul so brave ; because

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: καὶ τοῦ δικαίου · τλημονεστάτην δὲ σὲ
sections.translation: ’ tis right ; and thou hast shown more courage than any of thy sex my eyes have ever seen . Now , if thou hast aught to say to these children or thy aged guide , oh [*] ! say the last thou hast to say — then go .

sections.label: Section 574
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρε , πρέσβυ , χαῖρε καὶ δίδασκέ μοι
sections.translation: Farewell , old friend , farewell ! and prithee teach

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: τοιούσδε τούσδε παῖδας , ἐς τὸ πᾶν σοφούς ,
sections.translation: these children to be like thyself , wise at every point ; let them strive no further , for that will suffice them . And seek to save them from death , even as thou art anxious to do ; thy children are we , thy care it was that nurtured us . Thou seest how I yield my bridal

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: διδοῦσαν ἀντὶ τῶνδε κατθανουμένην .
sections.translation: bloom to die for them . For you , my brothers gathered here , may you be happy ! and may every blessing be yours , for the which my blood shall pay the price ! [*] Honour this old friend , and her that is within the house ,

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: τιμᾶτε πατρὸς μητέρʼ Ἀλκμήνην ἐμοῦ
sections.translation: Alcmena , the aged mother of my sire , and these strangers too . And if ever heaven for you devise release from trouble and a return to your home , remember the burial due to her that saved you , a funeral fair [*] as I deserve ; for I have not failed ,

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: ὑμῖν παρέστην , ἀλλὰ προύθανον γένους .
sections.translation: but stood by you , and died to save my race . This shall be my pearl of price instead of children , and for the maiden life I leave , if there be really aught beyond the grave — God grant there may not be ! For if , e ’ en there , we who are to die shall find a life of care ,

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδ ’ ὅποι τις τρέψεται · τὸ γὰρ θανεῖν
sections.translation: I know not whither one shall turn ; for death is held a sovereign cure for every ill .

sections.label: Section 597
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ μέγιστον ἐκπρέπουσʼ εὐψυχίᾳ
sections.translation: Maiden of heroic soul , transcending all thy race , be sure the fame that thou shalt win from us , in life , in death , shall leave the rest of women far behind ;

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: καὶ χαῖρε · δυσφημεῖν γὰρ ἅζομαι θεάν ,
sections.translation: farewell to thee ! I dare not say harsh words of her to whom thou art devoted , the goddess-daughter of Demeter . [ Exit Macaria .] Children , I am undone , grief unnerves my limbs ; take hold and support me to a seat hard by , when ye have drawn my mantle o ’ er my face , my sons .

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ὡς οὔτε τούτοις ἥδόμαι πεπραγμένοις ,
sections.translation: For I am grieved at what hath happened , and yet , were it not fulfilled , we could not live ; thus were the mischief worse , though this is grief enough .

sections.label: Section 1.608
sections.original: οὔτινά φημι θεῶν ἄτερ ὄλβιον , οὐ βαρύποτμον ,
sections.translation: Without the will of heaven none is blest , none curst , I do maintain ;

sections.label: Section 1.610
sections.original: οὐδὲ τὸν αὐτὸν ἀεὶ βεβάναι δόμον
sections.translation: nor doth the same house for ever tread the path of bliss ; for one kind of fortune follows hard upon another ; one man it brings to naught from his high estate , another though of no [*] account it crowns with happiness .

sections.label: Section 1.615
sections.original: μόρσιμα δʼ οὔτι φυγεῖν θέμις , οὐ σοφί-
sections.translation: To shun what fate decrees , is no wise permitted ; none by cunning shall thrust it from him ; but he , who vainly would do so , shall have unceasing trouble .

sections.label: Section 1.618
sections.original: ἀλλὰ σὺ μὴ προπίτνων τὰ θεῶν φέρε , μηδʼ ὑπεράλγει
sections.translation: Then fall not prostrate thou , but bear what heaven sends , and set a limit

sections.label: Section 1.620
sections.original: φροντίδα λύπᾳ ·
sections.translation: to thy soul ’ s grief ; for she , poor maid ! in dying for her brothers and this land , hath won a glorious death , and splendid fame shall be her meed from all mankind ;

sections.label: Section 1.625
sections.original: ἁ δʼ ἀρετὰ βαίνει διὰ μόχθων .
sections.translation: for virtue ’ s path leads through troublous ways . Worthy of her father , worthy of her noble birth is this conduct . And if thou dost honour the virtuous dead , I share with thee that sentiment .

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: ὦ τέκνα , χαίρετʼ · Ἰόλεως δὲ ποῦ γέρων ;
sections.translation: All hail , ye children ! Where is aged Iolaus ? where the mother of your sire , absent from their place at this altar ?

sections.label: Section 632
sections.original: πάρεσμεν , οἵα δή γʼ ἐμοῦ παρουσία .
sections.translation: Here am I , so far as I can be present at all .

sections.label: Section 633
sections.original: τί χρῆμα κεῖσαι καὶ κατηφὲς ὄμμʼ ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Why dost thou lie there ? Why that downcast look ?

sections.label: Section 634
sections.original: φροντίς τις ἦλθʼ οἰκεῖος , ᾗ συνειχόμην .
sections.translation: There is come a sorrow on my house , whereby I am distressed .

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: ἔπαιρέ νυν σεαυτόν , ὄρθωσον κάρα .
sections.translation: Arise , lift up thy head .

sections.label: Section 636
sections.original: γέροντές ἐσμεν κοὐδαμῶς ἐρρώμεθα .
sections.translation: I am old , and all my strength is gone .

sections.label: Section 637
sections.original: ἥκω γε μέντοι χάρμα σοι φέρων μέγα .
sections.translation: But I come with tidings of great joy for thee .

sections.label: Section 638
sections.original: τίς δʼ εἶ σύ ; ποῦ σοι συντυχὼν ἀμνημονῶ ;
sections.translation: Who art thou ? Where have I met thee ? I have no remembrance .

sections.label: Section 639
sections.original: Ὕλλου πενέστης · οὔ με γιγνώσκεις ὁρῶν ;
sections.translation: I am a vassal of Hyllus ; dost not recognize me now ?

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: ὦ φίλταθʼ , ἥκεις ἆρα σωτὴρ νῷν βλάβης ;
sections.translation: Best of friends , art thou come to save us twain from hurt ?

sections.label: Section 641
sections.original: μάλιστα · καὶ πρός γʼ εὐτυχεῖς τὰ νῦν τάδε .
sections.translation: Assuredly ; and moreover thou art lucky in the present case .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: ὦ μῆτερ ἐσθλοῦ παιδός , Ἀλκμήνην λέγω ,
sections.translation: Alcmena , mother of a noble son , to thee I call ! come forth , hear this welcome news . For long has anguish caused thee inwardly to waste , wondering if those ,

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ψυχὴν ἐτήκου νόστος εἰ γενήσεται .
sections.translation: who now are here , would ever come .

sections.label: Section 646
sections.original: τί χρῆμʼ ἀϋτῆς πᾶν τόδʼ ἐπλήσθη στέγος
sections.translation: What means that shout , that echoes throughout the house ? Hath there come yet a herald from Argos , O Iolaus , and is he treating thee with violence ? Feeble is any strength of mine ; yet thus much let me tell thee , stranger ,

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστʼ ἄγειν σε τούσδʼ ἐμοῦ ζώσης ποτέ .
sections.translation: never , whilst I live , shalt thou drag them hence . Shouldst thou succeed , no more let me be thought the mother of that hero . And if thou lay a finger on them , thou wilt struggle to thy shame with two aged foes .

sections.label: Section 654
sections.original: θάρσει , γεραιά , μὴ τρέσῃς · οὐκ Ἀργόθεν
sections.translation: Courage , aged dame , fear not ; not from Argos

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: κῆρυξ ἀφῖκται πολεμίους λόγους ἔχων .
sections.translation: is a herald come , with hostile messages .

sections.label: Section 656
sections.original: τί γὰρ βοὴν ἔστησας ἄγγελον φόβου ;
sections.translation: Why then didst raise a cry , fear ’ s harbinger ?

sections.label: Section 657
sections.original: σέ , πρόσθε ναοῦ τοῦδʼ ὅπως βαίης πέλας .
sections.translation: I called thee to come to me in front of this temple .

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: οὐκ ἴσμεν ἡμεῖς ταῦτα · τίς γάρ ἐσθʼ ὅδε ;
sections.translation: I know not what it means ; who is this ?

sections.label: Section 659
sections.original: ἥκοντα παῖδα παιδὸς ἀγγέλλει σέθεν .
sections.translation: A messenger who says thy grandson cometh hither .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: ὦ χαῖρε καὶ σὺ τοῖσδε τοῖς ἀγγέλμασιν .
sections.translation: All hail to thee for these thy tidings ! But why is he not here , where is he ? if in this land he hath set foot . What hath happened to keep him from coming hither with thee , to cheer my heart ?

sections.label: Section 664
sections.original: στρατὸν καθίζει τάσσεταί θʼ ὃν ἦλθʼ ἔχων .
sections.translation: He is posting the army he brought with him , and seeing it marshalled .

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: τοῦδʼ οὐκέθʼ ἡμῖν τοῦ λόγου μέτεστι δή .
sections.translation: Then have I no concern herein .

sections.label: Section 666
sections.original: μέτεστιν · ἡμῶν δʼ ἔργον ἱστορεῖν τάδε .
sections.translation: Yes , thou hast ; though it is my business to inquire into these matters .

sections.label: Section 667
sections.original: τί δῆτα βούλῃ τῶν πεπραγμένων μαθεῖν ;
sections.translation: What then wouldst thou learn of these events ?

sections.label: Section 668
sections.original: πόσον τι πλῆθος συμμάχων πάρεστʼ ἔχων ;
sections.translation: About how many allies has he with him ?

sections.label: Section 669
sections.original: πολλούς · ἀριθμὸν δʼ ἄλλον οὐκ ἔχω φράσαι .
sections.translation: A numerous force ; I cannot otherwise describe the number .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: ἴσασιν , οἶμαι , ταῦτʼ · Ἀθηναίων πρόμοι ;
sections.translation: The leaders of the Athenians know this , I suppose ?

sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: ἴσασι · καὶ δὴ λαιὸν ἕστηκεν κέρας .
sections.translation: They do ; already is their left wing set in array .

sections.label: Section 672
sections.original: ἤδη γὰρ ὡς ἐς ἔργον ὥπλισται στρατός ;
sections.translation: Is then the host already armed for battle ?

sections.label: Section 673
sections.original: καὶ δὴ παρῆκται σφάγια τάξεων ἑκάς .
sections.translation: Yea , and already are the victims brought [*] near [*] the ranks .

sections.label: Section 674
sections.original: πόσον τι δʼ ἔστʼ ἄπωθεν Ἀργεῖον δόρυ ;
sections.translation: About what distance is the Argive host from us ?

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: ὥστʼ ἐξορᾶσθαι τὸν στρατηγὸν ἐμφανῶς .
sections.translation: Near enough for their general to be plainly seen .

sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: τί δρῶντα ; μῶν τάσσοντα πολεμίων στίχας ;
sections.translation: What is he about ? marshalling the enemy ’ s line ?

sections.label: Section 677
sections.original: ᾐκάζομεν ταῦτʼ · οὐ γὰρ ἐξηκούομεν .
sections.translation: So we guessed ; we could not hear exactly . But I must go , for I would not that my master should engage the foe without me , if I can help it .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: κἄγωγε σὺν σοί · ταὐτὰ γὰρ φροντίζομεν ,
sections.translation: I also will go with thee ; for I like thee am minded , so it seems , to be there and help my friends .
